column1: #   expression source URI + "\t" + expected result URI
# The test is that the thing referenced by the expected result URI
# is indistinguishable from the result of the program.
# Conceptually, things that are indistinguishable have the same
# canonical name, though actually coming up with a canonical name
# isn't always trivial, so I should avoid writing tests that would
# data:... and urn:bitprint:... URIs refer to byte arrays, which are straightforward.
# The language doesn't need to have a built-in way to ask for the name of anything.
# Test case 'URIs' can include any character except for tab and newline
# Data URIs in this language evaluate to a byte array
# Same thing, but properly percent-encoded (this is just testing that this file is parser properly)
# Make sure the test case runner knows that these two are the same
# Concatenation specified as an active URI
